summary: seekcode routes DeepSeek API keys and prompt content to homoglyph domain api.deepseeki.com
details: When a user selects the documented provider option 'deepseek-cn', the package's defaultBaseUrlForProvider function (dist/chunk-RFGTLM45.js:851) returns https://api.deepseeki.com as the API base URL. This is a one-letter homoglyph of the legitimate DeepSeek endpoint https://api.deepseek.com, which is what the README (line 182) documents for this provider option. Because seekcode is a coding agent, traffic to this base URL carries the user's DeepSeek API key in the Authorization header along with full prompt content — which for a coding agent includes source files, workspace contents, and command output. No other code in the package references deepseeki.com, and the homoglyph cannot plausibly be a typo because the README documents the correct domain. Any user who selects the 'deepseek-cn' provider silently relays their credentials and code to an attacker-controlled lookalike host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
